_comment: OpenObserve v0.91.1 alert TEMPLATE + DESTINATION for the Keep webhook.
_lineage: Consumed by scripts/setup-monitoring.py. v2 alerts (alerts.json) reference the destination by name; the destination renders the template and POSTs it to Keep's /alerts/event endpoint. Verified shapes against live grove-obs.
_ssrf_note: OpenObserve's SSRF guard REJECTS a destination URL that resolves to a private IP at CREATE time — so `url` cannot be the internal keep-backend service name / private IP. The script fills `url` from KEEP_EVENT_URL, which must be an SSRF-allowed address that reaches Keep (obs droplet public IP with keep-backend published admin-only + firewalled — hairpin NAT verified; or loopback with ZO_SSRF_ALLOW_LOOPBACK=true). The X-API-KEY header value is filled from KEEP_WEBHOOK_TOKEN at runtime.
_placeholders: OO renders {alert_name} and {stream_name}/{org_name} natively; severity/runbook_key/tenant are carried per-alert in query context_attributes and rendered as {key}. Keep's workflows filter on `severity` and template {{ alert.name }}/{{ alert.message }}/{{ alert.runbook_key }}.
